title: In the Matter of Altaba Inc., f/d/b/a Yahoo! Inc. (2018) — SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure
technical_focus: Incident discovery and escalation to disclosure
Disclosure controls for cybersecurity risk and incidents
Materiality and timeliness of breach disclosure
legal_issues: Exchange Act reporting and Rule 13a-15 disclosure controls

timeline.event: Intrusion affecting hundreds of millions of user accounts (per SEC order findings)
Public disclosure of 2014 breach during sale process
SEC cease-and-desist order; $35 million civil penalty (neither admit nor deny)

primary_sources.label: SEC Order Instituting Cease-and-Desist Proceedings (File No. 3-18448) — PDF

evidence_artifacts: Security-to-finance escalation records for known incidents
Disclosure committee minutes and materiality worksheets
Draft 10-Q/10-K cybersecurity risk language with change history
Auditor and counsel consultation logs on incident reporting
engineering_takeaways: Security incidents must feed disclosure controls—not only operational IR.
Delayed escalation to legal and finance can become a securities compliance failure.
